item.title: Hilton to face Becerra in election for California governor, CBS News projects
item.description: Republican Steve Hilton, a former Fox News host and adviser to former British Prime Minister David Cameron, will advance to November's general election in the race to become California's next governor, CBS News projects.

item.title: New sunscreen option wins FDA approval after years of delay
item.description: A sunscreen ingredient that's been available in Europe, Japan and South Korea for years has finally been approved by the FDA for sale in the U.S.

item.title: FDA approves new sunscreen ingredient
item.description: The FDA approved a new ingredient for sunscreen that's been in use in Europe for years. Dr. Jon LaPook has more details.

item.title: Texas BBQ restaurants losing money on briskets due to cost of beef
item.description: Beef is so expensive right now that barbecue joints are losing money on brisket and trying to steer customers to other choices. CBS News' Jason Allen reports.

item.title: Platner seeks Democratic nomination to take on Sen. Collins in closely watched race
item.description: Maine Democrat Graham Platner is seeking to make the nomination official to take on Republican Sen. Susan Collins in November.

item.title: Nick Reiner seeks trust fund release from judge during trial of his parents' murder
item.description: Nick Reiner, who's accused of killing his parents, Rob and Estelle Reiner, last December, wants a judge to order the release of money from his trust fund. Carter Evans reports.

item.title: U.S. launches retaliatory strikes for downing of Apache helicopter
item.description: The two helicopter pilots were uninjured. After the retaliatory strikes were announced, President Trump said, "I believe the response should be very strong, very powerful."

item.title: House passes pro-union bill after 20 Republicans defy party
item.description: It's the seventh time this session that a discharge petition has secured the necessary 218 signatures to force a vote on legislation.

item.title: Questions about Graham Platner's past could impact Maine's U.S. Senate race
item.description: Voters headed to the polls in Nevada, South Carolina, North Dakota and Maine, which features a nationally-watched U.S. Senate race. As Democrats try to win back the Senate in November, their candidate in Maine has admitted to a sexting scandal and is now fighting off allegations from several former girlfriends. Caitlin Huey-Burns has more.

item.title: U.S. military says it fired new strikes in Iran after downing of Apache helicopter
item.description: The U.S. military said it carried out new strikes on Tuesday in Iran after the downing of a U.S. Apache helicopter by Iran. The crew was carried to safety aboard an American drone boat. Charlie D'Agata has more on the crew's rescue.

item.title: Emotions high as jury finds Karmelo Anthony guilty of murder in fatal track meet stabbing
item.description: Emotions were high after a jury in Texas found 19-year-old Karmelo Anthony guilty of murder in the fatal stabbing of 17-year-old Austin Metcalf at a high school track meet last year. Immediately after the verdict, tension erupted outside the courthouse. Jonah Kaplan reports.

item.title: Breaking down South Carolina's primaries with Trump's endorsement looming large in governor's race
item.description: South Carolina's governor's race is in focus on this primary day where Lt. Gov. Pamela Evette has the backing of President Trump in a crowded field that includes Rep. Nancy Mace. CBS News' Skyler Henry reports.

item.title: U.S. Apache helicopter shot down by Iran, Trump says; crew rescued by sea drone
item.description: The two crew members were rescued by a sea drone in the first such operation ever carried out by the U.S. military, officials told CBS News.

item.title: Maine Democrats considering Graham Platner for Senate nomination amid controversies
item.description: Voters in four states are casting their ballots in Tuesday's primaries, but most attention is on Maine's Democratic Senate primary where Graham Platner is favored to win despite a string of controversies. CBS News' Fin Gómez and Anthony Salvanto have more.

item.title: Bernie Sanders voices support for Graham Platner: "There are no saints in the U.S. Senate"
item.description: Sen. Bernie Sanders on Monday offered his support for Graham Platner, despite Platner's string of scandals. CBS News' Robert Costa reports.

item.title: Primary day wrapping up in Maine as voters grapple with Graham Platners' scandals
item.description: Democrats in Maine appear poised to officially choose Graham Platner as their nominee to take on Republican Susan Collins for her U.S. Senate seat. CBS News' Caitlin Huey-Burns and Robert Costa have more.

item.title: Trump stands by decision to name Pulte to intel role despite revolt in Congress
item.description: Bill Pulte will begin serving as acting director of national intelligence in a week and a half, President Trump said, effectively standing by his decision to name the housing regulator and Trump loyalist.

item.title: A timeline of the Karen Read case
item.description: A look at the timeline of events in Karen Read's high-profile Massachusetts murder trial and retrial. Read was acquitted of killing her boyfriend, Boston police officer John O'Keefe.

item.title: Full interview: NASA administrator Jared Isaacman on moon base plans, Artemis III, more
item.description: NASA administrator Jared Isaacman speaks with CBS News' Mark Strassmann about the upcoming Artemis III mission, its complexity, NASA's plans for an eventual moon base and more.

item.title: House passes $70 billion bill to fund ICE for rest of Trump's term
item.description: The House passed Republicans' $70 billion bill to fund immigration enforcement agencies for the rest of the Trump administration.

item.title: U.S. launches self-defense strikes against Iran, CENTCOM says, after Apache downing
item.description: U.S. Central Command says it began launching self-defense strikes Tuesday in response to Iran's shoot-down of a U.S. military helicopter patrolling the Strait of Hormuz. CBS News' Eleanor Watson and Natalie Brand have more.

item.title: Stabbing attack in Belfast sparks calls for anti-immigration protests
item.description: Police appeal for calm as graphic video of a Belfast stabbing attack fuels calls for anti-immigration protests in Northern Ireland.

item.title: NASA names 4 astronauts for next Artemis mission
item.description: NASA's Artemis III astronauts plan to carry out rendezvous and docking procedures with commercial moon landers being built by SpaceX and Blue Origin.

item.title: U.S. launches self-sefense strikes against Iran in response to chopper downing, CENTCOM says
item.description: The U.S. military says it began retaliatory strikes on Iran in response to the downing of an Apache helicopter. CBS News' Eleanor Watson has more.

item.title: FEMA overhaul would make disaster aid harder to access, analysts warn
item.description: A new analysis warns a proposed FEMA overhaul by a Trump-appointed panel would limit access to disaster aid for survivors, shifting the burden to state and local agencies amid hurricane season.

item.title: Watchdog finds waste and unsanitary conditions at ICE facility inside Fort Bliss
item.description: From August 2025 to March 2026, an immigrant detention facility at Fort Bliss Army base suffered a host of serious incidents, including two deaths that were investigated by the Government Accountability Office.

item.title: Karmelo Anthony found guilty of murder in stabbing of Austin Metcalf
item.description: The trial of Karmelo Anthony centered on whether he acted in self-defense when he fatally stabbed Austin Metcalf at a Frisco ISD track meet in 2025.

item.title: May inflation likely topped 4% for first time in 3 years, economists say
item.description: Economists expect the Consumer Price Index this week to show U.S. inflation continuing to rise due to higher energy costs.

item.title: U.S. bars entry of FIFA World Cup referee from Somalia
item.description: Federal immigration authorities barred a Somali soccer referee who was slated to officiate the FIFA World Cup from entering the U.S. over the weekend, citing "vetting concerns."

item.title: Consumer Reports names its top sunscreens for 2025
item.description: Consumer Reports tested 51 sunscreens to find the best options to protect your skin this summer and beyond.
